content.problemStatement:
Given an array A1,A2…ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1​,A2​…AN​, find the minimum number of operations (possibly zero) required to convert all integers in AAA to 000.

In one operation, you


choose a non-negative integer ppp (p≥0p \geq 0p≥0),
select at most KKK indices in the array AAA, and
for each selected index iii, replace AiA_iAi​ with Ai⊕2pA_i\oplus 2^pAi​⊕2p. Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR.
Subtasks
**Subtask #1 (100 points)**: Original Constraints

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN, KKK - the size of the array and the maximum number of elements you can select in an operation.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2…ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1​,A2​…AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations to make all elements of the array 000.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and K – the size of the array and the maximum number of indices you may select in one operation. The next line contains N integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum number of operations required to turn all array elements into zero.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Bit counts: p0→1, p1→3, p2→1, p3→1. With K=2 the needed operations are ceil(1/2)+ceil(3/2)+ceil(1/2)+ceil(1/2)=1+2+1+1=5.

First test case already all zeros, so 0 operations. In the second case each number has a distinct set bit, each bit appears once, K=3 allows clearing each bit in one operation, total 4 bits → 4 operations.
All numbers are 111 (bits 0,1,2). For each of the three bits count=5, K=10 so ceil(5/10)=1. Total 3 operations.

hidden.label: edge case K larger than N

solution.approach:
The operation flips a single bit (2^p) on at most K chosen elements. Bits are independent – flipping bit p never influences any other bit. For each bit position p we count how many array elements have this bit set (c_p). In one operation with this p we can clear at most K of those bits, therefore the minimum number of operations needed for this bit is ceil(c_p / K). The overall answer is the sum of these minima over all bit positions (0 … 30, because Ai ≤ 10^9). The algorithm:
1. Initialise an array cnt[31] with zeros.
2. For each Ai, iterate over bits 0..30 and increment cnt[p] when the p‑th bit of Ai is 1.
3. After processing the whole array, answer = Σ_{p=0}^{30} (cnt[p] + K - 1) / K (integer division).
Complexities are linear in N·log(maxAi).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N · B) per test case, where B = 31 (number of bits needed for 10^9).
